title: FieldDescriptor lacks mo_arg bounds validation
description:
File: python/src/lichen/schc/rules.py:70-74

FieldDescriptor.lsb_bits() computes:
  return self.length_bits - self.mo_arg

If mo_arg > length_bits, this returns a negative value. When used in BitWriter.write() or BitReader.read(), negative nbits would raise ValueError('nbits must be non-negative').

Similarly, _check_msb() in codec.py:111 computes:
  shift = fd.length_bits - fd.mo_arg

If shift is negative, the subsequent >> operation on Python would still work but produce wrong results (Python handles negative shifts differently than expected).

While rules are typically defined correctly at import time, there's no validation that prevents creating a FieldDescriptor with mo_arg > length_bits.

Fix: Add validation in FieldDescriptor.__post_init__ or lsb_bits():
  if self.mo_arg is not None and self.mo_arg > self.length_bits:
      raise ValueError(...)

Category: CORRECTNESS
